TitleStatutory Liens
URLhttps://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/02/25/2026-03758/statutory-liens
SummaryThe NCUA Board (Board) is publishing this proposed rule to remove a provision of NCUA regulations regarding federal credit unions' (FCUs) statutory lien authority. The Board believes it is redundant to continue to include a definition of the term "except as otherwise provided by law or except as otherwise provided by federal law" when it is axiomatic that a law that supersedes…
CategoryFederal Register
SourceFederal Register
Published2026-02-25T00:00:00Z
TitleCredit Union Service Contracts
URLhttps://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/02/25/2026-03757/credit-union-service-contracts
SummaryThe NCUA Board (Board) is proposing to revise its regulations governing the organization and operation of federal credit unions (FCUs) by eliminating a provision related to credit union service contracts. The Board intends to reduce administrative costs and compliance complexity with this revision, enabling FCUs to serve their members more efficiently.
CategoryFederal Register
SourceFederal Register
Published2026-02-25T00:00:00Z
TitleRefund of Interest
URLhttps://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/02/25/2026-03756/refund-of-interest
SummaryThe NCUA Board (Board) is issuing for public comment a proposal to rescind its regulation that addresses the refund of interest to members. This regulation is redundant, as it restates the authority already granted to a federal credit union's (FCU's) board of directors by the Federal Credit Union Act (FCU Act) section 113(9).
CategoryFederal Register
SourceFederal Register
Published2026-02-25T00:00:00Z
TitlePurchase, Sale, and Pledge of Eligible Obligations
URLhttps://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/02/25/2026-03755/purchase-sale-and-pledge-of-eligible-obligations
SummaryThe NCUA Board (Board) solicits public comment on a proposal to streamline its regulations governing the purchase, sale, and pledge of eligible obligations. The Board proposes to remove the prescriptive lists of items that must be addressed in the written policies adopted by a federal credit union (FCU). Although FCUs would still be required to maintain written policies, remov…
CategoryFederal Register
SourceFederal Register
Published2026-02-25T00:00:00Z
TitleCompensation in Connection With Loans to Members and Lines of Credit to Members
URLhttps://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/02/25/2026-03754/compensation-in-connection-with-loans-to-members-and-lines-of-credit-to-members
SummaryThe NCUA Board (Board) is issuing for public comment a proposal to amend the NCUA's regulation that limits a federally insured credit union (FICU) official and employee compensation in connection with loans to members and lines of credit to members. These regulations have generated confusion and are unduly restrictive. To provide clearer and more flexible standards, the propos…

SourceFederal Register
Published2026-02-25T00:00:00Z
TitlePost-Election Training for New Board Members
URLhttps://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/02/25/2026-03753/post-election-training-for-new-board-members
SummaryThe NCUA Board (Board) solicits public comment on a proposal to eliminate the regulatory requirement that each director of a federal credit union (FCU) attain a working familiarity with finance and accounting within 6 months after election or appointment. The Board believes the regulation is unnecessarily prescriptive.

TitleVisa to Acquire Prisma Medios de Pago and Newpay in Argentina
URLhttps://usa.visa.com/about-visa/newsroom/press-releases.releaseId.22176.html
SummaryBUENOS AIRES, Argentina --(BUSINESS WIRE)--Feb. 19, 2026-- Visa (NYSE: V) today announced that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Prisma Medios de Pago S.A.U. (“Prisma”) and Newpay S.A.U. (“Newpay”) in Argentina from Advent International.

TitleCVE-2026-0102 Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) Defense in Depth Vulnerability
URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-0102
SummaryUnder specific conditions, a malicious webpage may trigger autofill population after two consecutive taps, potentially without clear or intentional user consent. This could result in disclosure of stored autofill data such as addresses, email, or phone number metadata.

TitleCVE-2023-2804 Red Hat, Inc. CVE-2023-2804: Heap Based Overflow libjpeg-turbo
URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2023-2804
SummaryA heap‑based buffer overflow exists in libjpeg‑turbo’s h2v2_merged_upsample_internal() function when processing 12‑bit lossless JPEG images. An attacker could craft an image containing out‑of‑range 12‑bit samples that, when decompressed with merged upsampling enabled, may trigger a segmentation fault or buffer overflow, resulting in an application crash.
CategoryIS
SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-02-10T08:00:00Z
TitleCVE-2026-21255 Windows Hyper-V Security Feature Bypass Vulnerability
URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-21255
Summary**How could an attacker exploit this vulnerability?** Exploitation requires an attacker who already has local execution on a VBS‑enabled guest VM to run a specially crafted application or driver that abuses the hypervisor’s overlay handling to bypass VBS/VTL protections and compromise kernel integrity.
CategoryIS
SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-02-10T08:00:00Z

SummaryThe Federal Housing Finance Agency ("FHFA" or the "Agency") is issuing this final rule to repeal the Fair Lending, Fair Housing, and Equitable Housing Finance Plans regulation ("part 1293"). TitleAgency Information Collection Activities: Proposed Collection Renewal; Comment Request

SummaryThe Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), as part of its obligations under the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995 (PRA), invites the general public and other Federal agencies to take this opportunity to comment on the renewal of the existing information collection described below (OMB Control No. 3064-0139).
